rubrics: Facts should describe the rules, the cyclic win-loss structure, and the standard mixed-strategy equilibrium.
Facts should stay focused on the abstract game and not introduce variants, extra actors, or behavioral detours.
Facts should remain compact and proportional to a solved toy game.
All player entities should be genuine actors who independently choose actions in the game, not cognitive modes, internal mental processes, or rule enforcers
A standard 2-player game should have exactly 2 player entities
Should identify one game (RPS), not invent additional game variants unless the prompt explicitly asks about iterated play
Assumptions should be things that could genuinely be violated or questioned, not definitional properties of the game
Known theorems or proven results should not be listed as assumptions
Scenarios for a solved game with one Nash equilibrium should focus on the equilibrium outcome, not speculative behavioral forecasts or rule violations
